scenario.title: The Casino Vault
scenario.narrative: A casino vault was robbed. Logic: (Manager_Card OR Master_Key) AND Time_Safe. BCC would require 8 tests. You have a budget for exactly 4. Build a perfectly interlocked MC/DC suite.
scenario.code:
if (Manager_Card or Master_Key) and Time_Safe:
    open_vault()

claim: For many expressions, a minimal MC/DC suite can often be built with N+1 tests. This mission uses a 3-condition expression where 4 tests are enough.
charges: Select exactly 4 logs that form valid independence pairs for all 3 conditions.
Remember: You can reuse a log in multiple pairs!
correct_answer_explanation: Debrief: Masterful! You achieved MC/DC with only 4 tests. You used R7 (F,F,T) as a baseline 'False' test for both M and K. You used R3 (T,F,T) to prove M, and R5 (F,T,T) to prove K. Then you paired R3 with R4 to prove T. You proved independence for all 3 variables while saving the court 50% of the testing budget!
wrong_answer_explanation: That selection doesn't form valid independence pairs for all 3 conditions. Find a set of 4 that overlap efficiently.
hints: Prosecutor's Note: Start by finding a 'Base False' log like R7 (F,F,T). What happens if you flip just M? What happens if you flip just K?
Then find a pair to test T by flipping T while holding the others in a state that makes the door open.

misconceptions.explanation_md: MC/DC doesn't require 2 tests for EVERY condition uniquely. A single test can participate in multiple pairs! This is why a 3-variable decision often only needs 4 tests (N+1) instead of 8.
